skill.name: ralph-tui-prd
skill.description: Creates Product Requirements Documents for ralph-tui task orchestration. It asks focused clarification questions, captures quality gates, and produces PRDs with user stories for later conversion to issues or prd.json.
skill.summary: Generate structured PRDs for ralph-tui agent workflows.
skill.author: subsy

security_audit.summary: Static external command findings are false positives because the reported backticks are Markdown examples and inline command names, not executable Ruby or shell code. Static weak cryptography findings are also false positives because no cryptographic APIs or algorithm implementations appear at the reported lines. A medium workflow risk remains because the skill asks users to provide quality gate commands that may later be copied into agent execution plans.

medium_findings.title: User-Supplied Quality Gate Commands May Influence Later Agent Runs
medium_findings.description: The skill asks users to specify quality gate commands and then includes those commands in PRDs for ralph-tui workflows. The skill does not execute them directly, but downstream agents may treat the generated PRD as an execution plan, so malicious or unsafe commands could be propagated if a user or copied prompt supplies them.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence clearly shows that command strings are requested and embedded in PRD content. The risk depends on later tools or agents executing the generated instructions, so it is a workflow risk rather than direct code execution.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Execution Alerts Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick findings occur inside Markdown examples, code fences, PRD markers, or inline command references. No executable script, Ruby backtick expression, or automatic command invocation is present in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is a Markdown instruction document and the detected backticks are formatting delimiters or inline examples. I found no shell execution syntax that would run from the skill file itself.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Keyword Collisions
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography locations do not contain cryptographic code, hashing calls, encryption APIs, or algorithm selection. They are ordinary PRD instructions and example text, so these alerts are false positives.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the reported lines found product requirements language, not cryptographic implementation. The signal appears to come from static keyword matching rather than semantic evidence.

content.value_statement: Planning agent-ready features is hard when requirements are vague or too large. This skill guides clarification and produces structured PRDs for ralph-tui execution workflows.
actual_capabilities: Asks iterative clarification questions before drafting a PRD.
Requires quality gate commands for each planned user story.
Produces PRDs wrapped in parsing markers for ralph-tui.
Structures requirements into goals, user stories, criteria, and non-goals.
Writes user stories sized for focused AI agent sessions.
Includes open questions and technical considerations when needed.
limitations: Does not implement the requested feature.
Does not validate whether quality gate commands are safe.
Depends on user answers for project context and scope.
Targets ralph-tui workflows rather than general product planning.

use_cases.title: Plan a New Feature
use_cases.description: Turn an initial feature idea into a structured PRD with goals, user stories, and acceptance criteria.

use_cases.title: Prepare Agent Work
use_cases.description: Create small, independent stories that can be assigned to AI coding agents through ralph-tui.

use_cases.title: Define Quality Gates
use_cases.description: Capture the typecheck, lint, test, and visual verification commands that each story must satisfy.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic PRD
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning for a clear feature idea.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a PRD for adding [feature] to [project]. Ask me clarifying questions first, including quality gate commands.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a UI Feature
prompt_templates.scenario: Feature planning that needs browser or visual verification.
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a ralph-tui PRD for [UI feature]. Include questions about affected screens, accessibility, persistence, and browser verification.

prompt_templates.title: Break Down a Complex Change
prompt_templates.scenario: Planning a feature that spans multiple components.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a PRD for [complex change]. Split it into independent user stories that one AI agent session can complete.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare an Agent Execution Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced planning for conversion into ralph-tui tasks.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a detailed PRD for [initiative]. Include goals, non-goals, technical considerations, success metrics, open questions, and project-specific quality gates.

output_examples.input: Create a PRD for adding dark mode to the TUI.
output_examples.output: The assistant asks about goals, persistence, affected components, quality commands, and visual verification. It then returns a marked PRD with overview, goals, quality gates, stories, requirements, non-goals, metrics, and open questions.

output_examples.input: Plan a feature for importing tasks from a CSV file.
output_examples.output: The assistant clarifies file format, validation behavior, error handling, and tests. The final PRD includes small stories for parsing, previewing, validating, importing, and reporting errors.

output_examples.input: Spec out better failure recovery for agent runs.
output_examples.output: The assistant explores recovery goals, retry behavior, state storage, and operator controls. The PRD separates recovery state, retry rules, interface changes, and audit logging into independent stories.

best_practices: Provide project context before requesting the final PRD.
Use safe, familiar quality gate commands from the repository.
Keep each user story small enough for one focused agent session.
anti_patterns: Do not ask it to implement the feature after drafting the PRD.
Do not include destructive shell commands as quality gates.
Do not skip clarification when scope or success criteria are unclear.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates Product Requirements Documents formatted for ralph-tui task orchestration.

faq.question: Does it write code?
faq.answer: No. The skill explicitly focuses on creating the PRD, not implementing the feature.

faq.question: Why does it ask about quality gates?
faq.answer: Quality gates define the commands and checks that each user story must satisfy later.

faq.question: Can I use it outside ralph-tui?
faq.answer: Yes, but its output markers and story structure are designed for ralph-tui parsing.

faq.question: Is it compatible with Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What should I review before using the PRD?
faq.answer: Review scope, acceptance criteria, and every quality gate command before downstream agents use the PRD.
